body.processed:
How can I use Bibliotek.dk?

At Bibliotek.dk  you can find and order materials from Danish libraries and pick them up at your local library.

Once you have ordered the material at Bibliotek.dk, the order is sent to your local library, which takes care of the order. You have to be a registered user at the pickup library.

You can borrow almost everything

You can borrow most of Bibliotek.dk's materials. Though, there are exceptions. The libraries set their own lending rules and can decide:

  • only to lend books to its own users, e.g. NOTA
  • that a particular collection is not for loan, e.g. The Royal Library's collection of movies

Bibliotek.dk does not purchase materials

Bibliotek.dk contains at the same time all materials at Danish libraries and the Danish national bibliography. The national bibliography is a registrant of all published materials in Denmark - music, books, films etc. It is all displayed at Bibliotek.dk - even if it should not be available in a library.

Bibliotek.dk does not purchase materials and we do not have influence on the libraries' choice of materials. If you have suggestions to a new material at Bibliotek.dk, please contact your local library.

Who is behind Bibliotek.dk?

DBC Digital  develops and operates Bibliotek.dk.

(KL) Kommunernes Landsforening (KL - Local Government Denmark) is overall responsible for Bibliotek.dk.

1508 has made the graphic design for Bibliotek.dk

What can you use Bibliotek.dk for?

Bibliotek.dk's users, both private persons and companies, can freely use data from Bibliotek.dk for their own use (i.e. internal use for companies). If you want to use data commercially, you need an agreement on rights of use. Please contact  DBC Digital

Contact us

You are always welcome to contact us: DBC Digital's customer service

Ask a librarian

If you are looking for help with literature search, subject search, or factual questions, you can contact the inquiry service  “Ask a librarian” (Danish). They aim to respond within 24 hours. Questions submitted on weekends and holidays will be answered on the following weekday.

Fee, renewal and purchase proposal

If you have questions about fees, renewal, or acquisition of materials, please contact your local library.

Website functions and technology

For questions and comments about changes/errors in the registration of materials, functions, and technology on Bibliotek.dk, you can contact DBC Digital’s customer service. First, check if there is an answer to your question on the list of frequently asked questions or under Help and instructions.

About our Privacy Policy
In this section you can read about how the information you leave when using the website is used by Bibliotek.dk and the libraries.  

If you have questions concerning the Privacy Policy please contact our customer service Kundeservice or ask at your local library if your question regards the library’s use of information.

Here you can read about which personal data is used by Bibliotek.dk and how:

Personal information (name, CPR-number, mail address etc.)

Your information is encrypted when you order through Bibliotek.dk. That is done to prevent unauthorized disclosure, and your information can only be read by sender and receiver. In some cases, you will be redirected to your local library which means the local library are responsible for handling the information you give to them.
 

When you order materials through Bibliotek.dk information is stored in two places:

  • In a database containing orders at DBC Digital
  • Locally with the library that handles the order.


Information is handled in strict confidence by both DBC Digital and the libraries.

The library staff follow the rules of confidentiality with regards to information about your library use.

Mails between DBC Digital and the libraries with information concerning your order will be encrypted.

Encryption and security certificate

When you submit personal information via Bibliotek.dk the information is encrypted so only you and the receiving party can read them.

Bibliotek.dk uses security protocol (TLS - Transport Layer Security) which creates a secure connection between you as a user of Bibliotek.dk and DBC Digital as provider of Bibliotek.dk.

A secure connection guarantee:

  • data is exactly as you send them – they cannot be altered during transmission
  • data is kept secret for others than the intended recipient.


The TLS-function is stored in your browser. It is activated automatically when you type personal information while ordering materials.

You do not need to install extra software to use the library safely.

What is stored and where?

The following information is stored by Bibliotek.dk (at DBC Digital):

  • Order information. Personal data is deleted one month after your  ”Date of interest”
  • Statistics on use of Bibliotek.dk (without personal data)


Order information is sent to the library you have chosen.

Your rights

According to the Data protection regulation a user have certain rights:

The right to see personal information

You have the right to see the personal information we handle about you.

The right to correct information
You have the right to correct or modify incorrect information.

The right to erase information
In some cases you can get personal information erased before the time of automatic deletion.

The right to limited processing
In some cases you have the right to restrict the processing of your personal data. If you have the right to restricted processing, we can only process information – except storage – after having collected your consent, or in order to establish, ascertain or defend legal requirements, or in order to protect a person or important societal interests.

The right of objection
In some cases you have the right to make an objection to our legal handling of your personal information. You can also make an objection to your personal information being used in direct marketing.

Read more about your rights at The Danish Data Protection Agency  

File a complaint to The Danish Data Protection Agency
You can file a complaint to The Danish Data Protection Agency if you are dissatisfied with the way we process and handle your personal information. Contact The Danish Data Protection Agency

If you have questions regarding your rights please contact our data protection advisor: dpo@dbc.dk

​What does Bibliotek.dk contain?

At Bibliotek.dk you will find all items that have been published in Denmark and all items found in the stock of the Danish public libraries. These items can be borrowed from the libraries.
The database is updated daily. Among other things, Bibliotek.dk contains records of:

  • items from the stock of the Danish public libraries and research libraries: books, periodicals, newspapers, music, sheet music, video tapes/DVDs, CD-ROMs, audio books etc
  • all books, periodicals and newspapers published in Denmark since 1970 (as well as many items published before this date)
  • major articles published in newspapers and periodicals since 1945
  • reviews published in newspapers and periodicals since 1990

Please note that Bibliotek.dk preferably contains Danish newspaper and journal articles.


Why cannot I borrow every material?

In Bibliotek.dk there are some materials that can not be borrowed - or can only be borrowed by some:
Online materials: Some of the online materials are only available if your library has purchased access
Restricted Rights: Libraries have restricted access to some materials - especially movies. The materials may for example only be lent to a municipality's own citizens.
New materials, that are not available in the libraries yet

Bibliotek.dk contains preferably major Danish articles from 1945 and reviews in newspapers and magazines from 1949.

You can find all materials from Danish libraries in Bibliotek.dk - articles, books, films, music, sheet music and games. If you want to search a particular category, choose from the drop box next to the search field or from the list above the search field.

 

Type your keyword - or the beginning of the word. You will get suggestions as you type.

In this example a search on Libanon:

When you write a keyword, you'll get suggestions matching your search term. For each suggestion, you'll be able to see the format: Book, subject or something else.

If you choose "libanon" as a subject, you will search all materials, which are tagged with the subject "libanon".

The search result

In the search result you will see a list of the hits matching your search enquiry.

In the short display you see the material's title, author, material format and cover:

In the detailed display you'll get the following possibilities:

  • ordering the material
  • choose between the formats
  • see reviews
  • see all editions.
     

Click to see reviews, details, editions, etc.

 

Subject search

Further search from a material

Use also the keywords in a material for further search. Type the chosen keyword and search:

Related subjects

In most search results (ion Danish) you will get supplementary subjects to inspire to further search.

Here was searched for "klimapolitik":

You also get supplementary subjects in the posts:

The difference between the two types of subject terms is that the first group of terms, Keywords, are words that are related to the content of the book.

The second group, Related tags, are subject terms that are related to the book's keywords, but which are not directly represented in the book.

To order materials at Bibliotek.dk you have to be a member of a Danish public library. You do not have to create a user account at Bibliotek.dk.

If you are not already a member of a public library, please follow these steps:

  • Go to the front page of Bibliotek.dk and select “Sign in” at the top of the screen to the right
  • Select “Ny bruger” next to the “Log ind”-button
  • Choose your library from the list and go to your library's website
  • Sign up using your MitId - and now you can use Bibliotek.dk.
     

Log in at Bibliotek.dk

  • Click sign in
  • Choose your library from the list
  • Type your CPR number
  • Type your pincode (four or five digits - the one you use at your local library)

At Bibliotek.dk you can order materials from all Danish libraries and pick them up - and return them - at your local library.

Good idea to log in

We recommend that you log in, so that your data is present when you order.

Find the material, you want to order and click the Order button.

How to order magazines and articles

When ordering a magazine, you have to type the year and number, you want.

Find the magazine, you want to order, for instance National Geographic.

To order, please fill in volume/year and number, if you know it. Click "Select copy or article".

Type volume/year and number of the magazine - if you know the number. Save.

If you want a particular article, please click "Only interested in a particular article?" Fill in as much information as possible. Save.

See more about articles in the help text How to find articles

Change pick up library

You can change pickup library while ordering

If your mail address is not visible, please fill in the input field. Some libraries might need more information. Click Accept to finish your order.

Editions

We have collected all editions of a material in the same place. This means that you immediately get an overview of which material types a material has been published in and how many editions have been published.

At the top of the page you can see all available material types, eg. Bog (Book), Lydbog (Audio Book) and E-bog (E-book).

Scroll down to see the editions in a list. Each edition can be expanded in order to see more data.

 

List of content

In collections of eg. music and poetry there might be a lot of content. You get an overview of this under Content. If there are more than 10 titles in the list, the rest are expanded in a window.

body.processed:
It is easy to find articles in Bibliotek.dk.

From the start of your search you can choose the category: "articles".

A search for "great britain" results in articles from both journals and newspapers.

If the article is available online, you can easily open it from the record:

 

Find articles via the journal
If you want an overview of articles in a specific journal, you can find them like this:

Search for the journal by typing its title in the search field.
TIP: If you get too much noise, it might help to also type "journal". For example, "psychology journal".

Open the record and scroll down the page.

Here you’ll find an overview of available years and topics:

Select a year or topic. Once you’ve selected a year, the list of topics will be narrowed down to match the selected year.

If you want to search all articles, click on "Search the articles". Click "Edit" and fill in the fields to search within the journal’s articles.

Even more overview
You can also get an overview of a specific journal issue via an article.

In the article, you’ll find information about where the article comes from, typical topics in the journal, and an overview of all articles in the same issue:

Access to digital articles

Many articles are available online.

In some cases, you will be asked to log in to get access, but many articles are available with just one click.

More ways to access online articles:

You can access online articles in several ways in Bibliotek.dk

Some of them require that you log in, others are accessible with just one click.
When you click “All order options”, a window presenting all ways to the article opens.

You may be offered these online options

  • Direct access at the publisher's site
  • DBC Web Archive, which offers a pdf version of the article
  • Retriever article
  • Digital copy of the article from The Danish Royal Library


This article can be accessed in three different ways:

Digital copy

You can only order a digital copy, if your library subscribes to the service Digital Artikelservice. So you have to be logged in to order a digital copy. To order, click the button Order digital copy and follow the instructions.

More information

Under Information and editions you can see more information about the article, e.g. in which journal it was published:

It is easy to find reviews of materials, you find in Bibliotek.dk

When you have found the material, scroll down to the reviews.

The reviews are shown in this order:

  1. The libraries' assessments (lektørudtalelser)
  2. Reviews from Litteratursiden
  3. Reviews from newspapers and magazines with star rating
  4. Reviews from newspapers and magazines without star rating

Many reviews are accessible online. Most reviews from newspapers are only accessible, if you are logged in. Your library has to subscribe to the web service Retriever to allow you to see newspaper reviews.

Others are also available in pdf-format.

In Bibliotek.dk, you can choose between two ways to search - basic and advanced, depending on your task at hand.

Basic search

In many cases, the basic search will be sufficient.

Write in the search box what you are looking for. It can be a title, an author, a topic, or a mix. If you remember part of a title, the author’s first name, and that it was a book - just write that, for example:

  • "to kill harper book"
  • "murakami women"
  • "beatles abbey"
  • "quiet girl dvd"

 

 

If you know which material type you are looking for, you can choose material type up front to only search for i.e. films

In the search results, you will have several options to continue working with your search.

Use the filters to refine by material type, physical/digital material, language, fiction or non-fiction, and more.

The order of hits in the search result depends on several factors in the underlying ranking. It is continuously adjusted, but the following rank highly:

  • popularity
  • new materials
  • Danish materials
  • exact hits

Advanced search

As a student or researcher, you may need to do more advanced searches. Go to the advanced search page. Click Advanced next to the search field.

Field search

You can now search using field search, where you compose the search using fields connected by the operators AND, OR, NOT.

Be aware that if you choose to edit your search in CQL the changes will not appear in the boxes in field search. Once you start editing in the CQL-field you will not be able to switch back to field search. 

 

Truncation

If you are not quite sure of the ending of your keyword or just want to search broadly, you can truncate your keyword with an asterisk *. When you truncate, you search at the beginning of the word and any ending. This means that searching for climate* searches for all words that start with climate: Climate change, climate policy, climate research, etc.

 

Further Limitations

You can narrow down your search to a specific time interval, a particular language or material type and age group using the dropdown boxes at the bottom of the advanced search page.

Command Search

Go to "Edit in CQL" in order to search using search operators. The search operators are specially developed for the search method used for advanced search.

 

Please note, that many search terms must be in Danish, since the indexes are only developed in Danish.

Here you have a list of the most often used search operators:

 

  • term.subject=epigenetik and term.creator="ida donkin"
  • term.title="pussy riot" and worktype=movie
  • term.title="your man" and term.creator=cohen

When searching in Bibliotek.dk, the number of search hits is not displayed. And you don’t need to know the number: The ranking of the search result means that you get the most relevant search hits on the first pages.

The search method (fuzzy search) searches on several parameters and therefore returns hits very broadly. When searching, you are not only searching for the exact search term, but also for terms close by. Therefore, the full search result becomes so large that it does not make sense to show the number of hits.

If you need to know the exact number of search hits, we recommend that you use the advanced search. You will find it by selecting Advanced next to the search field.

body.processed:
How can I use Bibliotek.dk?

At Bibliotek.dk  you can find and order materials from Danish libraries and pick them up at your local library.

Once you have ordered the material at Bibliotek.dk, the order is sent to your local library, which takes care of the order. You have to be a registered user at the pickup library.

You can borrow almost everything

You can borrow most of Bibliotek.dk's materials. Though, there are exceptions. The libraries set their own lending rules and can decide:

  • only to lend books to its own users, e.g. NOTA
  • that a particular collection is not for loan, e.g. The Royal Library's collection of movies

Bibliotek.dk does not purchase materials

Bibliotek.dk contains at the same time all materials at Danish libraries and the Danish national bibliography. The national bibliography is a registrant of all published materials in Denmark - music, books, films etc. It is all displayed at Bibliotek.dk - even if it should not be available in a library.

Bibliotek.dk does not purchase materials and we do not have influence on the libraries' choice of materials. If you have suggestions to a new material at Bibliotek.dk, please contact your local library.

Who is behind Bibliotek.dk?

DBC Digital  develops and operates Bibliotek.dk.

(KL) Kommunernes Landsforening (KL - Local Government Denmark) is overall responsible for Bibliotek.dk.

1508 has made the graphic design for Bibliotek.dk

What can you use Bibliotek.dk for?

Bibliotek.dk's users, both private persons and companies, can freely use data from Bibliotek.dk for their own use (i.e. internal use for companies). If you want to use data commercially, you need an agreement on rights of use. Please contact  DBC Digital

Contact us

You are always welcome to contact us: DBC Digital's customer service

Bibliotek.dk does not purchase materials and we do not have influence on the libraries' choice of materials. If you have suggestions to a new material at Bibliotek.dk, please contact your local library.

Who is behind Bibliotek.dk?

DBC Digital  develops and operates Bibliotek.dk.

(KL) Kommunernes Landsforening (KL - Local Government Denmark) is overall responsible for Bibliotek.dk.

1508 has made the graphic design for Bibliotek.dk

What can you use Bibliotek.dk for?

Bibliotek.dk's users, both private persons and companies, can freely use data from Bibliotek.dk for their own use (i.e. internal use for companies). If you want to use data commercially, you need an agreement on rights of use. Please contact  DBC Digital

Contact us

You are always welcome to contact us: DBC Digital's customer service

About our Privacy Policy
In this section you can read about how the information you leave when using the website is used by Bibliotek.dk and the libraries.  

If you have questions concerning the Privacy Policy please contact our customer service Kundeservice or ask at your local library if your question regards the library’s use of information.

Here you can read about which personal data is used by Bibliotek.dk and how:

Personal information (name, CPR-number, mail address etc.)

Your information is encrypted when you order through Bibliotek.dk. That is done to prevent unauthorized disclosure, and your information can only be read by sender and receiver. In some cases, you will be redirected to your local library which means the local library are responsible for handling the information you give to them.
 

When you order materials through Bibliotek.dk information is stored in two places:

  • In a database containing orders at DBC Digital
  • Locally with the library that handles the order.


Information is handled in strict confidence by both DBC Digital and the libraries.

The library staff follow the rules of confidentiality with regards to information about your library use.

Mails between DBC Digital and the libraries with information concerning your order will be encrypted.

Encryption and security certificate

When you submit personal information via Bibliotek.dk the information is encrypted so only you and the receiving party can read them.

Bibliotek.dk uses security protocol (TLS - Transport Layer Security) which creates a secure connection between you as a user of Bibliotek.dk and DBC Digital as provider of Bibliotek.dk.

A secure connection guarantee:

  • data is exactly as you send them – they cannot be altered during transmission
  • data is kept secret for others than the intended recipient.


The TLS-function is stored in your browser. It is activated automatically when you type personal information while ordering materials.

You do not need to install extra software to use the library safely.

What is stored and where?

The following information is stored by Bibliotek.dk (at DBC Digital):

  • Order information. Personal data is deleted one month after your  ”Date of interest”
  • Statistics on use of Bibliotek.dk (without personal data)


Order information is sent to the library you have chosen.

Your rights

According to the Data protection regulation a user have certain rights:

The right to see personal information

You have the right to see the personal information we handle about you.

The right to correct information
You have the right to correct or modify incorrect information.

The right to erase information
In some cases you can get personal information erased before the time of automatic deletion.

The right to limited processing
In some cases you have the right to restrict the processing of your personal data. If you have the right to restricted processing, we can only process information – except storage – after having collected your consent, or in order to establish, ascertain or defend legal requirements, or in order to protect a person or important societal interests.

The right of objection
In some cases you have the right to make an objection to our legal handling of your personal information. You can also make an objection to your personal information being used in direct marketing.

Read more about your rights at The Danish Data Protection Agency  

File a complaint to The Danish Data Protection Agency
You can file a complaint to The Danish Data Protection Agency if you are dissatisfied with the way we process and handle your personal information. Contact The Danish Data Protection Agency

If you have questions regarding your rights please contact our data protection advisor: dpo@dbc.dk

body.processed:
​What does Bibliotek.dk contain?

At Bibliotek.dk you will find all items that have been published in Denmark and all items found in the stock of the Danish public libraries. These items can be borrowed from the libraries.
The database is updated daily. Among other things, Bibliotek.dk contains records of:

  • items from the stock of the Danish public libraries and research libraries: books, periodicals, newspapers, music, sheet music, video tapes/DVDs, CD-ROMs, audio books etc
  • all books, periodicals and newspapers published in Denmark since 1970 (as well as many items published before this date)
  • major articles published in newspapers and periodicals since 1945
  • reviews published in newspapers and periodicals since 1990

Please note that Bibliotek.dk preferably contains Danish newspaper and journal articles.


Why cannot I borrow every material?

In Bibliotek.dk there are some materials that can not be borrowed - or can only be borrowed by some:
Online materials: Some of the online materials are only available if your library has purchased access
Restricted Rights: Libraries have restricted access to some materials - especially movies. The materials may for example only be lent to a municipality's own citizens.
New materials, that are not available in the libraries yet

Bibliotek.dk contains preferably major Danish articles from 1945 and reviews in newspapers and magazines from 1949.

You can find all materials from Danish libraries in Bibliotek.dk - articles, books, films, music, sheet music and games. If you want to search a particular category, choose from the drop box next to the search field or from the list above the search field.

 

Type your keyword - or the beginning of the word. You will get suggestions as you type.

In this example a search on Libanon:

When you write a keyword, you'll get suggestions matching your search term. For each suggestion, you'll be able to see the format: Book, subject or something else.

If you choose "libanon" as a subject, you will search all materials, which are tagged with the subject "libanon".

The search result

In the search result you will see a list of the hits matching your search enquiry.

In the short display you see the material's title, author, material format and cover:

In the detailed display you'll get the following possibilities:

  • ordering the material
  • choose between the formats
  • see reviews
  • see all editions.
     

Click to see reviews, details, editions, etc.

 

Subject search

Further search from a material

Use also the keywords in a material for further search. Type the chosen keyword and search:

Related subjects

In most search results (ion Danish) you will get supplementary subjects to inspire to further search.

Here was searched for "klimapolitik":

You also get supplementary subjects in the posts:

The difference between the two types of subject terms is that the first group of terms, Keywords, are words that are related to the content of the book.

The second group, Related tags, are subject terms that are related to the book's keywords, but which are not directly represented in the book.

To order materials at Bibliotek.dk you have to be a member of a Danish public library. You do not have to create a user account at Bibliotek.dk.

If you are not already a member of a public library, please follow these steps:

  • Go to the front page of Bibliotek.dk and select “Sign in” at the top of the screen to the right
  • Select “Ny bruger” next to the “Log ind”-button
  • Choose your library from the list and go to your library's website
  • Sign up using your MitId - and now you can use Bibliotek.dk.
     

Log in at Bibliotek.dk

  • Click sign in
  • Choose your library from the list
  • Type your CPR number
  • Type your pincode (four or five digits - the one you use at your local library)

At Bibliotek.dk you can order materials from all Danish libraries and pick them up - and return them - at your local library.

Good idea to log in

We recommend that you log in, so that your data is present when you order.

Find the material, you want to order and click the Order button.

How to order magazines and articles

When ordering a magazine, you have to type the year and number, you want.

Find the magazine, you want to order, for instance National Geographic.

To order, please fill in volume/year and number, if you know it. Click "Select copy or article".

Type volume/year and number of the magazine - if you know the number. Save.

If you want a particular article, please click "Only interested in a particular article?" Fill in as much information as possible. Save.

See more about articles in the help text How to find articles

Change pick up library

You can change pickup library while ordering

If your mail address is not visible, please fill in the input field. Some libraries might need more information. Click Accept to finish your order.

Editions

We have collected all editions of a material in the same place. This means that you immediately get an overview of which material types a material has been published in and how many editions have been published.

At the top of the page you can see all available material types, eg. Bog (Book), Lydbog (Audio Book) and E-bog (E-book).

Scroll down to see the editions in a list. Each edition can be expanded in order to see more data.

 

List of content

In collections of eg. music and poetry there might be a lot of content. You get an overview of this under Content. If there are more than 10 titles in the list, the rest are expanded in a window.

body.processed:
It is easy to find articles in Bibliotek.dk.

From the start of your search you can choose the category: "articles".

A search for "great britain" results in articles from both journals and newspapers.

If the article is available online, you can easily open it from the record:

 

Find articles via the journal
If you want an overview of articles in a specific journal, you can find them like this:

Search for the journal by typing its title in the search field.
TIP: If you get too much noise, it might help to also type "journal". For example, "psychology journal".

Open the record and scroll down the page.

Here you’ll find an overview of available years and topics:

Select a year or topic. Once you’ve selected a year, the list of topics will be narrowed down to match the selected year.

If you want to search all articles, click on "Search the articles". Click "Edit" and fill in the fields to search within the journal’s articles.

Even more overview
You can also get an overview of a specific journal issue via an article.

In the article, you’ll find information about where the article comes from, typical topics in the journal, and an overview of all articles in the same issue:

Access to digital articles

Many articles are available online.

In some cases, you will be asked to log in to get access, but many articles are available with just one click.

More ways to access online articles:

You can access online articles in several ways in Bibliotek.dk

Some of them require that you log in, others are accessible with just one click.
When you click “All order options”, a window presenting all ways to the article opens.

You may be offered these online options

  • Direct access at the publisher's site
  • DBC Web Archive, which offers a pdf version of the article
  • Retriever article
  • Digital copy of the article from The Danish Royal Library


This article can be accessed in three different ways:

Digital copy

You can only order a digital copy, if your library subscribes to the service Digital Artikelservice. So you have to be logged in to order a digital copy. To order, click the button Order digital copy and follow the instructions.

More information

Under Information and editions you can see more information about the article, e.g. in which journal it was published:
